steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence opens with an introductory participial phrase (“Rising 11,371 feet above sea level”) and the subject (“Mount Leuser”). After the blank comes the prepositional phrase (“among the world's tallest mountains”).

steps.title: Choose the correct verb form
steps.content: An independent clause must have a finite verb as its main verb. “Is ranked” (choice A) is a finite verb in passive voice. The other choices—”to rank” (infinitive), “ranking” (present participle), and “having ranked” (past participle)—are non-finite verb forms that cannot function as the main verb of an independent clause. That matches choice A — A is correct.
